description_long: This object is a Mesa/Boogie Dual Rectifier guitar amplifier head, featuring a robust rectangular chassis with a black exterior and a distinctive gold diamond-plate metal faceplate. The front panel is equipped with numerous control knobs, switches, and indicator lights, allowing for detailed sound customization. The top of the unit has a sturdy handle for portability and a vented grille for heat dissipation. The rear panel reveals multiple large vacuum tubes, various input/output jacks, and additional switches, indicating its function as a high-powered tube amplifier. The sides and top are plain, with a matte black finish and minimal detailing. This amplifier head is designed to be placed on top of a speaker cabinet and is commonly found in music venues, studios, or garages.
description: A Mesa/Boogie Dual Rectifier guitar amplifier head with a gold diamond-plate faceplate, black chassis, and visible vacuum tubes on the back. It features numerous control knobs and a sturdy handle on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gold diamond-plate faceplate, control knobs, switches, and indicator lights.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain black side panel and the top handle.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing multiple vacuum tubes, input/output jacks, and additional switches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain black side and top handle.
